//: CI-gated checkJs pass over shipped JS. Tests stay covered by ESLint (incl. type-aware promise rules via tsconfig.eslint.json); templates ship to the user's env where their optional deps exist, so they can't typecheck here.
//strict: This gate inherits `strict: false` from tsconfig.eslint.json — which is why the flags below are enabled one at a time instead of via `strict: true`. Every one was MEASURED at zero errors before being turned on, so each is a ratchet: it pins a property the codebase already has and cannot silently lose. The three still off are the ones with real work behind them, with their exact cost as of 2026-07-20: noImplicitAny (877 errors), useUnknownInCatchVariables (67), strictNullChecks (37 — the smallest, and the one most likely to surface real null-deref bugs, so the best next candidate). strictPropertyInitialization cannot be set without strictNullChecks. To re-measure one: tsc -p a config that extends this and adds the single flag.
